user_query:
You’re my restaurant operations analyst. We operate a 120-seat casual dining restaurant in Austin, TX. Please read input/restaurant_context.json and input/menu_items.csv, then produce four deliverables under output/:

1) output/menu_engineering.csv — Classify each menu item into Star, Plowhorse, Puzzle, or Dog using this matrix: Stars = food cost % < 30 and menu mix % > 15; Plowhorses = food cost % > 30 and menu mix % > 15; Puzzles = food cost % < 30 and menu mix % < 15; Dogs = food cost % > 30 and menu mix % < 15. Include columns: Item, Price, FoodCostPercent, MenuMixPercent, Category.

2) output/targets.json — Include target ranges for our concept (Casual Dining): food_cost_target, labor_cost_target, prime_cost_target. Use industry-standard target ranges and store values as strings with the percent ranges.

3) output/revenue_forecast.csv — Build a 12-month forecast using US-average seasonal indices. Include columns: Month (January..December), IndexLow, IndexHigh, IndexMidpoint, ForecastRevenue. For IndexLow/High, use the typical ranges for each month. Compute IndexMidpoint as the midpoint of that range. Compute ForecastRevenue = base_monthly_revenue * (IndexMidpoint / 100), using base_monthly_revenue from input/restaurant_context.json. Round ForecastRevenue to the nearest dollar.

4) output/ops_plan.md — A concise but complete plan with: (a) Menu engineering actions for each category (how to promote Stars, fix Plowhorses, reposition Puzzles, and remove/replace Dogs), (b) Casual dining cost benchmarks (food, labor, prime) and a brief prime-cost plan, (c) FOH staffing by shift for 120 seats using the per-50-seat model (show math, then give final recommended headcounts for lunch, dinner, and weekend peak), (d) Revenue per sq ft assessment for our space using our square footage and benchmarks, (e) 3–5 critical KPIs with formulas (include RevPASH) and example calculations using our seating and hours data from input/restaurant_context.json, and (f) a brief health inspection prep checklist covering the top recurring violations.

If you need any assumptions, state them clearly at the top of output/ops_plan.md. Write only the requested files to output/.
